reasoning: The 12-month CD national deposit rate has shown a clear downward momentum over the past five months (falling from 1.84 in Nov 2024 to 1.78 in Mar 2025). This tracks the broader macroeconomic environment of the Fed's rate-cutting cycle and easing monetary policy. While the FRED tool's linear estimate gave a 68% chance of being above 1.78 by late April, this relies on a naïve ordinary least squares (OLS) regression that predicts a mean reversion to 1.813 simply because the historical 1-year mean is much higher than the current value. In reality, interest rates act closer to a random walk with drift, and the current drift is negative roughly -0.01 or -0.02 per month. Because banks continue to adjust their funding costs downward in a rate-cutting environment, the probability of the rate jumping above the current 1.78 value (or 1.80 if Feb's value is taken as the baseline) is relatively low in the short term. I have assigned a low probability of an increase by April, with the uncertainty steadily growing toward 50% for the dates further out (reflecting the possibility that the Fed might pause cuts or resume hiking by early 2026).
